message:
Comprehensive edge-case and correctness fixes across dtype, ndarray, random, IO, and ufunc for NumPy behavioral parity

dtype (fnp-dtype):
- Return F64 from min_scalar_type for values exceeding i64/u64 range
  instead of incorrectly returning I64/U64

io (fnp-io):
- Eliminate memory leaks in loadtxt error paths that used Box::leak to
  create &'static str error messages; replace with static string literals

ndarray (fnp-ndarray):
- Handle zero known_product correctly in fix_unknown_dimension: allow
  reshaping empty arrays (0 elements) to shapes containing zeros
- Fix as_strided bounds check to use the actual view's byte span
  (required_view_nbytes with current strides) instead of the total
  contiguous buffer size, which was too permissive for strided views

random (fnp-random):
- Use wrapping_sub/wrapping_add for integer range generation to prevent
  panics when the range spans the i64 sign boundary (e.g. i64::MIN to
  i64::MAX)

ufunc (fnp-ufunc):
- Remove manual floor_divide zero-handling; rely on IEEE 754 semantics
  to match NumPy behavior for 0/0 and x/0 cases
- Fix np.sign to preserve negative zero (-0.0) by returning x instead
  of literal 0.0
- Replace manual banker's rounding with f64::round_ties_even() for
  Round and Rint operations
- Fix ULP spacing for f64::MAX (no next float exists; compute backward)
- Use saturating_add for i64 diagonal offset arithmetic in
  tri/tril/triu/eye/tril_indices/triu_indices to prevent overflow panics
- Use saturating_sub for variance/std ddof divisor to prevent underflow
- Add bounds-checking with descriptive errors for delete, insert, and put
- Fix rot90 to use swapaxes(0,1) instead of transpose(None) for correct
  >2D array behavior
- Rewrite apply_along_axis outer-loop indexing to handle zero-size
  dimensions and empty arrays correctly
- Add empty-array and overflow guards to bincount
- Allow zero-size results in einsum by removing .max(1) on output_size
- Return errors for all-NaN slices in nanargmin/nanargmax instead of
  silently returning index 0
- Use saturating_sub in pad_edge and diag to prevent underflow on
  zero-size dimensions
- Validate zero-size axes in pad_wrap cannot be wrapped with non-zero
  padding
- Early-return for zero-length axis in cumsum

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
